mnemonics: CRUD on a Chain (Create, Read, Update, Delete) - Visualize performing these fundamental operations on a linked chain of data.
keyPoints: Traversal requires starting at the head and following the 'next' pointers until the desired node or the end (null) is found.
Insertion and deletion at the head are O(1) operations, as they only require updating the head pointer and the new node.
Insertion and deletion at a specific node require traversal to find the target's predecessor, making them O(n) operations in the worst case.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the 'next' pointer of the previous node when deleting a node, causing a broken link and potential memory leak.
Attempting to access the 'data' or 'next' member of a null pointer, often after traversing past the end of the list.
Improperly handling the special case of inserting at or deleting the head node, which requires updating the list's head reference.
